in its own family of planets. And all this form a milky way, the galaxy called the milky
way, and like that the billions of galaxies. So what we know of this universe, only out
of pride, only when we are deluded, bewildered by illusory energy, we can say that, we can
confidently say, my understanding of the world is perfect. Otherwise, even if somebody
is humble, and uses common sense, one will be open-minded, will be not very rigid about
his views in terms of who is the creator. Some so-called learned scientists have said
that, if you are a scientist, you are a scientist, you are a scientist, you are a scientist,
very confidently propound their theories. Again, maya parita jnana, Krishna says, they
may be intelligent, but maya has stolen away their intelligence.
So our scriptures here, very nicely they are describing, and how it tallies with the present
day understanding. Sometimes we may say, it may not tally with what the present day
science says, it is quite possible that as, just like there was a time, there was a time
when science said that the earth is flat, but all along our scriptures say bhugol, gol
means ghramanda, anda. So there could be, as, since we are students of Bhagavatam, Bhagavad
Gita, all authorized scriptures, sometimes we may also get bewildered when we may hear
something which, according to scientists, is contradictory.
Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita,
Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita.
Convicting what is mentioned here in the Bhagavad Gita. But we should have confidence that
these scriptures are apaurushaya, they are not man-made.
This knowledge is given by the Lord and it is not subject to the four defects. The four
defects are, we all have cheating propensity, we have limited sense perception.
We commit mistakes and we are subject to illusion.
Recently in the newspapers, you would have read about a very big car maker company, Volkswagen.
Millions and millions of cars worldwide.
We are talking about cheating propensity.
They, so to say, installed a software which faked, or so-called cheated, the emission testing process.
Because each car has to be subject to emission tests and all that.
And in millions, now they have filed a suit.
The company, the dam.
The dam is worth 19 billion dollars.
So cheating propensity, it can be at that scale.
One of the best brands in the market.
Millions of cars being sold all over the world.
And the brains behind that car, they want to cheat.
So it can happen with the scientists.
There is a book written by some of the devotees called Forbidden Art.
It is a book about the history of archaeology.
How many of the so-called facts which emerge when the scientists are trying to prove or trying to find out the truth.
Or some evidences which come up.
How they have been set aside if they don't tally with the profound theories, present day theories.
They deliberately put under the carpet.
They deliberately put under the carpet.
They deliberately put under the carpet.
They deliberately put under the carpet.
So that way we can understand that with these inherent four defects,
the world which we are living, the knowledge which is being taught,
is also contaminated with these defects.
Sometimes knowingly, sometimes unknowingly.
Sometimes intentionally, sometimes unintentionally.
So here it is being nicely described that how this is a science.
In fact, there is a...
In fact, there is a...
There is a Vedic literature called Pindasiddhi
where the scientific understanding of pregnancy is very nicely described.
Like we mentioned in one of the earlier discussion that
how the Vedic wisdom, the Vedic knowledge, the Vedas is not only about God.
It is a body of knowledge which deals with every aspect of human existence.
Including defending.
There is scripture which talks about...
There is scripture which talks about weaponry.
Defense.
There is scriptures which talk about Ayurveda.
How to keep the body heal and healthy.
It is a body of knowledge.
And today why Ayurveda is becoming popular because
seeing various kinds of side effects and this thing of modern medicine,
find that how it is more reasonable or rather more better.
To have holistic, to subscribe to holistic medicine or holistic lifestyle.
So here we see that how Jaya and Vijaya being cursed,
they are taking birth as twins to Prajapati Kashyapa.
And in continuation to what we discussed in my previous...
We had touched upon that, that how
just like Jaya and Vijaya are devotees of the Lord
and not ordinary devotees.
To be doorkeepers of Vaikuntha,
personal attendants to the Lord, it is not an ordinary thing.
And we see that how Jaya and Vijaya
committed a mistake
and how they were cursed.
And because of that they had to take birth as
Hirnakashyapu and Hirnakshah here.
So as devotees we can also
have some important lessons from this past time.
We may be very nicely situated.
When we see people around us, when we see
people outside practicing Krishna consciousness,
various kinds of struggles,
we may see that yes, we are far more favorably situated.
We are staying in the association of devotees.
We have an opportunity to engage full time in the service of the Lord.
We have an opportunity to every day get up in the morning, attend Mangala Aarti, attend Bhagavatam class.
All the programs.
It's a great, good fortune.
For somebody who is trying to practice Krishna consciousness outside,
even to get an opportunity like this,
it's not easy.
Even so-called many initiated devotees, maximum they are very,
sometimes the pressures of life or the jobs are so high that
even if one is able to complete sixteen rounds, it's a big deal.
So to get five hours, six hours
of time where we can focus completely
on remembering the Lord, chanting His holy names,
attending Aarathis, doing various kinds of spiritual activities,
it's a big luxury.
So we are in this situation,
but we cannot be complacent.
We cannot take it for granted.
Just like Jaya Vijaya.
This material world is,
Prabhupada says, practicing spiritual life is like walking on a razor's edge.
Then Prabhupada describes razor's edge means small in attention and there is blood.
Although these days razors have improved,
we don't see much blood because everything is,
but typical barber razor which they use,
I mean if, we see many times we get cuts also.
So,
small in attention and it's blood.
Same is the case in the spiritual life.
Grossly and subtly.
Grossly could be somebody who could lose devotional service.
He can go out of company of association of devotees.
That's gross.
Even subtle, subtle fall down can happen.
Small mistake and your good consciousness which we had is gone.
We are back to material consciousness.
Material calculations, material worries, material anxieties.
After good morning sadhana,
we are, so to say, propelled.
The consciousness is propelled into higher dimension.
Our thinking is, quote unquote,
we experience bliss.
Our thoughts are sublime.
And we make a blunder, small blunder, moments inattention.
Krishna bhulia jiva bhoga bhancha kare,
nikatastha maya tare jhapatiya dhare.
Jhapatiya, it's not something which happens
nikatastha, maya is there, right there.
Jhapatiya dhare, jhapati means it
pounds on you.
So that way, we cannot be complacent.
Just like Jaya and Vijaya.
They didn't, they were not arrogant.
They were not, so to say,
just they came, the Kumaras came, a mistake happened.
And they got cursed.
And see what they are going through.
Born as demons.
No doubt, we have to have a
rider that it's all the pastime of the Lord.
So, even during Prabhupada's time,
many devotees,
when they were practicing Krishna consciousness,
unfortunately,
were trapped by maya.
So Prabhupada says, regarding Damodara's plight,
Damodara was a devotee,
we were very sorry, we are very sorry,
but we must always expect that some of the weak soldiers
may fall down in a fight
waged against the great
maya, external energy of Krishna.
So spiritual life is fighting
a battle, battle between
on one side is maya,
the allurement of maya,
the other side is Krishna.
Right now, we haven't
got the taste.
We are more attracted to the
illusionary energy of the Lord.
bhogaishwarya prasaktanam
tayyaparita cetasam
That's our present state.
Why do we get attracted to maya,
or the illusionary display of maya,
the variegatedness in this material world?
Because we all are
amsa of the Lord,
and our constitutional position is
sat chit ananda.
We are blissful,
there is a sense of permanence
because we are the atma, which is eternal,
there is no fear of death,
we are eternal,
because we are blissfully
amsa of the Lord, which is eternal,
there is no fear of death,
chit,
and full knowledge,
knowledge about our constitutional position,
knowledge about
we being amsa of the Lord,
and Lord being our protector,
fearless,
abhaya.
That's our constitutional position,
that's what we are meant to experience.
But see our present state now,
asat,
always fearful,
fearful of death,
various kinds of insecurities,
various kinds of fears,
various kinds of ignorance,
so much body of knowledge,
so much research is happening because
we are finding that
because of various kinds of limitations,
ignorance,
various kinds of
ajnaan,
ignorance,
we are not in control of things.
Various diseases torment us
because we don't have knowledge
how to counter those diseases.
So achit
and nirananda,
we are not happy.
So what maya does,
is when the jiva,
krishna bhulya jiva bhoga vancha kare,
when jiva wants to enjoy,
minus krishna,
iccha dvesha samuthena dvandamohena bharata,
first thing maya does is,
pulls krishna,
pulls the living entity's attention
from krishna's domain,
thinking of krishna,
consciousness of krishna,
understanding that I am amsa of krishna,
I am meant for pleasure of krishna.
It pulls that understanding,
covers that understanding.
Maya has two aspects,
covering potency and bewildering,
avanatmika and prakshapatmika shakti.
So first it covers that real knowledge,
real understanding,
that we are meant for pleasure of krishna,
we are amsa of krishna,
it covers that knowledge,
pulls our attention from that domain,
krishna's domain,
and the next step is,
takes that attention and puts in
illusory variegatedness,
which is a perverted reflection
of variegatedness in the spiritual world.
In present time we see lot of variegatedness,
lot of allurements,
various sense objects,
everything looks real.
That's why in spite of we wanting
not to gratify our senses,
simply we get pulled,
because the attraction is real.
That is maya,
takes that attention and puts it in the variegatedness,
which is perverted reflection,
so to say replica of the original pleasure
in the spiritual world.
That original pleasure is replicated,
but in a perverted way,
it is illusion,
just like water and a mirage.
So all along we see in this world,
you tell somebody who does not have
this understanding,
or who has not practiced spiritual life,
that there is no happiness in the world,
then you feel that you are crazy.
Or you feel that,
you know, why are you unnecessarily
depriving yourself of happiness?
What's wrong in being happy?
What's wrong in enjoying life?
Doesn't make sense to them,
because we all are meant for enjoyment,
that's the constitutional position,
you cannot deny,
spirituality doesn't deny happiness.
Anandamayo bhyasat.
And the other side,
we say don't enjoy.
When we get this spiritual understanding,
we understand that,
yes, enjoyment is not denied,
but there is illusory enjoyment,
which we are getting cheated with.
Maya is cheating us with illusory enjoyment,
but we are entitled for real enjoyment.
So when we have come to spiritual life,
there is always a battle,
on one side we have allurement of maya,
the enjoyment which appears very real,
which is very glaring,
and we have taste for that,
we are bewildered,
bhogaishwara prasaktana,
our intelligence,
real intelligence has been stolen,
the other side is real enjoyment
for which we have no taste.
We have no faith.
It's mythology.
Krishna is mythology,
spiritual world is mythology.
So here it is said,
and maya's greatest weapon
is the sex desire,
attraction to the opposite sex.
So we cannot expect that all our soldiers,
some of the soldiers may fall down
and fight, wage against the great maya,
external energy of Krishna.
The sex attraction is very great
in the material world,
but in the spiritual world it is different.
So sex attraction,
it's there even in the spiritual world.
What we see here,
opposite sex in the forms,
it's a replica.
From Srimad Bhagavatam we know that
the opposite sex, namely the female members
of the vaikuntha are many many times
more beautiful than
they are in the heavenly planets.
We all heard of Apsaras and
the hips and attractive smiles
cannot stimulate sex urge,
passion of the man or male devotees.
In Vaikuntha also,
they are great,
you know, like here it says,
female members of Vaikuntha.
But the attractive smiles cannot stimulate.
Why it cannot stimulate?
Because the jiva who is there in the spiritual world
is experiencing real Sat Chit Ananda.
There is no requirement for
sending out your tentacles
to derive Sat Chit Ananda
from external sources.
In relationship with the Lord,
mamahi vamso jiva loke,
the jiva is experiencing
completeness.
Today we are having a deficit of
Sat, Chit and Ananda.
And because of that deficit
we are having hope that
I'll get here,
I'll get here,
I'll get there.
That's why the whole material existence
is to bridge the gap
which is the gap of the deficit
using various material environments.
But they cannot stimulate sex urge,
passion of the man or male devotees.
In the Vaikuntha planet,
the opulence is very great
yet they are all engaged
in the transcendental loving service of the Lord.
Sometimes people say that
spiritual life means
we should not be amidst opulence.
In Vaikuntha,
so much of opulence is there.
But they are not bewildered.
bhogaishwarya prasaktana
because they are connected to the Lord,
they are conscious of the Lord.
It is very difficult to conquer over
the influence of maya,
but one who is firmly fixed
in Krishna consciousness,
when we are fixed in Krishna consciousness,
the deficit of Sat Chit Ananda
proportionately comes down.
The more and more we are Krishna conscious,
the more and more we are
situated in our constitutional position
of being Sat Chit Ananda.
But who is firmly fixed in Krishna consciousness
cannot be victim of maya.
Anyway, we cannot stop our fighting with maya.
Now, the present state is
we are not Krishna conscious.
We are allured by maya.
We are practicing sadhana bhakti.
And while we are not pure,
we are engaging in various preaching activities,
meeting various people,
dabbling with various things.
Anyway, we cannot stop our fighting with maya.
Neither we can expect that
all our soldiers will remain unhurt.
It's a fight.
Fight with maya.
And maya is very powerful.
mamamaya dhuraptaya.
So in that sense,
we cannot be complacent.
Any time we cannot be complacent.
Any time maya can overpower us.
But that does not mean that
we should live in a state of fear or uncertainty.
If we are taking the medicine,
if we are following the process,
Srila Prabhupada in many many places mentions,
avashe rakshive krishnae vishwasa palana.
Although maya may be very very strong,
but if we have taken strong shelter of krishna's lotus feet,
following the process diligently,
then maya cannot harm us.
You know Arjuna's son Abhimanyu was killed
in the battle in which Bhishma Deva was party.
Fight Krishna's cause sincerely
and you will be glorified with victory.
Devotees generally don't fall down,
but in circumstances,
but if circumstantially they do,
the Lord because of their strong attachment to Him
gives them protection in all circumstances.
So this is a very nice balance.
On one side we have to be very cautious.
We have to be alert.
We cannot be complacent.
Other side we should not be leading a life out of fear, anxiety.
Sometime devotees have this thing,
you know,
what is the guarantee that I will be safe,
so better let me secure myself now or do now.
What if I fall down in future?
These kind of thoughts can cross.
Chitraketu fell down and became a demon,
but he too was protected.
Thus even if one falls down from the path of bhakti yoga,
one is ultimately saved.
So there is no harm.
One who is interested in his own salvation is not,
now somebody may go to the other extreme that
I will not take any risk.
Going out, meeting people,
now we see all kinds of billboards,
it's all very risky.
Or preaching,
sometimes we have to encounter,
deal with Mataji's.
It's very risky.
So somebody can say,
okay, nothing doing all this.
So one who is interested in his own salvation,
yes,
one can be very self-centered,
concerned only about his salvation,
his spiritual advancement,
but that's not the mission of Prabhupada.
One who is interested in his own salvation
is not as advanced in Krishna consciousness
as one who feels compassion for others
and who therefore propagates the Krishna consciousness movement.
Such an advanced devotee will never fall down,
for Krishna will give him special protection.
We are today standing not because of our own spiritual strength,
because,
Prabhupada,
one more place says,
Lord Chaitanya promises,
because we have to do so much managerial work
to propagate this Krishna consciousness movement,
Lord Chaitanya promises that we will not fall down.
So that protection of the Acharyas,
that protection of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu,
protection of Krishna is there with us.
Yes, if we make blunders,
very grossly,
very grossly,
don't follow their instructions,
take all kinds of risk,
then,
we are helpless.
That is the sum and substance of this Krishna consciousness movement.
It is my duty to deliver you the right things in right earnestness
and it's the duty of the receiver to act on the standard spiritual regulation.
When you left us,
I simply prayed to Krishna for your return to Krishna consciousness
because that was my duty,
Prabhupada is writing.
Any good soul who approaches me once for spiritual enlightenment
is supposed to be depending on my responsibility
to get him back to Krishna,
back to home.
When we take initiation,
it's a very serious commitment.
On one side,
we promise to follow the regulated principles,
on the other side,
the spiritual master takes the responsibility to deliver the disciple.
The disciple may misunderstand a bona fide spiritual master,
so under pressure of Mayas,
sometimes we misunderstand,
we may not have faith in his instructions.
Again, it's because of Maya's influence.
But a bona fide spiritual master never let go as a devotee once accepted.
Even a disciple misunderstands a bona fide spiritual master,
the master regrets for his inability to protect the disciple
and sometimes he cries with tears in the eyes.
So, right now we are not able to see that battle,
we are not able to see how we are overpowered by Maya,
we are not able to see the tricks of Maya.
But spiritual master can easily see that,
because he is not in illusion.
So when he sees that the Maya has taken away some of his disciples,
or tricked some of his disciples,
he feels very sorry.
We had an experience while my Guru Maharaj was alive,
one of his disciples who had accepted sannyas
was one day forcibly dragged by his wife.
My Guru Maharaj lamented with tears in his eyes,
saying that he could not save the soul.
So this is some insubstance that on one side we are practicing spiritual life,
it's a war on Maya,
Maya is very powerful,
but if we somehow pray to the Lord,
stick on to his lotus feet,
follow the process given by the Acharyas diligently,
then their protection is guaranteed.
And if we are protected by them,
if we have their blessings,
just like Prabhupada said,
it's my responsibility.
It's a dual thing,
one side we have to follow the principles,
we have to obey the instructions,
the other side,
the spiritual master takes the responsibility.
So if we do that,
then we don't have to have fear,
we can practice spiritual life,
and in due course of time,
the influence of Maya,
the allurements which we fall prey to,
will subside.
In a conditioned state,
in a conditioned state sometimes,
we say, I am very lusty.
Actually,
the soul is not lusty.
Conditioning means,
Maya has covered the real knowledge,
and helplessly we are getting attracted to Maya.
The whole spiritual process is
to undo that conditioning,
and revive our constitutional,
original pure consciousness.
It cannot happen overnight.
Till such time,
we will get attracted.
Till such time,
there will be habitual responses to sense objects.
There will be some occasional fall downs.
Just like Krishna says,
you have the right to do your duty,
you are not entitled to the results of your activities.
We have to see,
am I taking the medicine sincerely?
Am I following the process diligently?
We may sometimes even break the regulative principles,
helplessly.
That's a result.
That's an outcome.
Sometimes we get very disappointed and depressed.
Still Maya is attacking me.
Still I am falling down.
It will happen.
Only check we should be,
am I taking the medicine sincerely?
Am I seriously following the process?
Or am I doing some short circuits?
Am I, just like today we read,
am I partially following the process?
Just like you go to a doctor,
the doctor says,
take this course for ten days,
you take for one day,
and you say,
leave it.
Enough.
So if we are following the process,
even though we may be overpowered by Maya,
we come in illusion,
we fall down occasionally,
we should get up,
have courage,
have conviction that,
Prabhupada is with us.
If we follow his instructions,
he will protect me.
Avashya Rakshivya Krishna.
E Vishwasa.
We don't have to live in fear and anxiety.
Nor we should go to the other extreme of being complacent.
So this right balance,
if we do that,
we can practice this spiritual life
and come out of this illusion.
Let's stop here.
Vrahmat Raj.
